headline: Greg Abel Era At Berkshire Ties Leadership Shift To Valuation Gap
summary: Greg Abel has formally stepped into the CEO role at Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A), succeeding Warren Buffett. Abel has outlined portfolio management changes, including his first personal purchase of $15 million of Berkshire stock. The company has resumed share repurchases after a pause of nearly two years and fully exited its position in Kraft Heinz. Berkshire Hathaway enters this leadership shift with a share price of $747,800.01 and a long record as a diversified holding company. Over...

headline: Nestlé USA enters at-home condiments space with new sauce brand
summary: Minor’s Kitchen is the food giant’s first brand to be launched in America in two years and builds on growing demand for home cooking.
